segments.text: [music]
Ever needed a dashboard for your project
that displays key indicators, metrics,
and trends? A dashboard that you can
customize to your specific needs? Let's
dive into a project and explore the new
project dashboards feature available
with Sonar Cube Cloud Enterprise.
Here you will see the dashboards tab and
below that tab you will find two things.
The built-in project health dashboard
and all dashboards.
The project health dashboard is a sonar
built-in dashboard covering the
essential views and indicators for your
projects.
It covers security,
reliability
and maintainability.
You can drill down further if you want
to examine the details behind a
particular metric.
But if you wish to create your own
custom project dashboard, first head to
all dashboards. And here you will see a
list of all the dashboards that have
already been created.
You also have the option to learn more
about viewing and managing dashboards in
the Sona docs.
To create a custom dashboard, you have
two options. Select an existing
dashboard and edit it or start from
scratch. I've chosen to start from
scratch.
Enter a name and description for your
custom dashboard. This then creates the
new dashboard and it gets added to the
list. And from here, you can begin to
build. Enter edit mode and you can add
widgets and sections.
Let's say we want to understand how the
number of issues in my project is
trending over time.
I can build a visualization of the
count. I can select the metric to be
issue count
and I can also choose to add filters for
example overall code or just new code
and the various software qualities and
status. I can also add a trend
indicator.
Now let's add a line chart to this
dashboard to visualize this data over
time. I select line chart. I select my
metric. I can then add the filters I
want and I can also add a legend to the
chart
and it gets added to the dashboard. I
can then choose to arrange the widgets
as I want. You can also choose to create
a specific section in your dashboard.
You may, for example, want to have a
group of related metrics and indicators.
In this example, I'm going to create a
section that focuses just on security.
In this dedicated section, I'll add some
widgets that help me understand the
security posture of this project at a
glance. I'll speed things up. So first
of all, I'll add a count with a trend
for security issues. Next, I add a donut
chart. Again, looking at issue count and
their severity, but filtered by security
issues. And finally, I'll add a third
widget. Again, a donut chart that this
time we'll look at issue count
by language and filtered by security and
add them to the dashboard.
Finally, save the changes and you're
good to go. We will continue to add
widgets and enhancements to dashboards.
There's a link in the description if you
want to request a particular widget.
Enjoy creating your project dashboards.
Happy coding.

fullText: [music] Ever needed a dashboard for your project that displays key indicators, metrics, and trends? A dashboard that you can customize to your specific needs? Let's dive into a project and explore the new project dashboards feature available with Sonar Cube Cloud Enterprise. Here you will see the dashboards tab and below that tab you will find two things. The built-in project health dashboard and all dashboards. The project health dashboard is a sonar built-in dashboard covering the essential views and indicators for your projects. It covers security, reliability and maintainability. You can drill down further if you want to examine the details behind a particular metric. But if you wish to create your own custom project dashboard, first head to all dashboards. And here you will see a list of all the dashboards that have already been created. You also have the option to learn more about viewing and managing dashboards in the Sona docs. To create a custom dashboard, you have two options. Select an existing dashboard and edit it or start from scratch. I've chosen to start from scratch. Enter a name and description for your custom dashboard. This then creates the new dashboard and it gets added to the list. And from here, you can begin to build. Enter edit mode and you can add widgets and sections. Let's say we want to understand how the number of issues in my project is trending over time. I can build a visualization of the count. I can select the metric to be issue count and I can also choose to add filters for example overall code or just new code and the various software qualities and status. I can also add a trend indicator. Now let's add a line chart to this dashboard to visualize this data over time. I select line chart. I select my metric. I can then add the filters I want and I can also add a legend to the chart and it gets added to the dashboard. I can then choose to arrange the widgets as I want. You can also choose to create a specific section in your dashboard. You may, for example, want to have a group of related metrics and indicators. In this example, I'm going to create a section that focuses just on security. In this dedicated section, I'll add some widgets that help me understand the security posture of this project at a glance. I'll speed things up. So first of all, I'll add a count with a trend for security issues. Next, I add a donut chart. Again, looking at issue count and their severity, but filtered by security issues. And finally, I'll add a third widget. Again, a donut chart that this time we'll look at issue count by language and filtered by security and add them to the dashboard. Finally, save the changes and you're good to go. We will continue to add widgets and enhancements to dashboards. There's a link in the description if you want to request a particular widget. Enjoy creating your project dashboards. Happy coding.
